Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Understand the Goal The objective is to analyze two POWL models representing a Hire to Retire process identify anomalies deviations from standard logic and determine which model is closer to a normative version of the process

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Analyze the Standard Hire to Retire Process What is the typical flow Post Job Advertisement Screen Candidates Conduct Interviews Make Hiring Decision Onboard Employee Add to Payroll Close Case Key Dependencies You cannot interview before screening usually You cannot decide before interviewing You cannot onboard before deciding to hire You cannot add to payroll before onboarding or at least before hiring decision Screening and Interviewing are usually sequential not parallel Payroll is mandatory for a hired employee

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Analyze POWL Model 1 Structure StrictPartialOrder Nodes Post Screen Decide Interview Onboard Payroll Close Edges Post Screen Screen Decide Screen Interview Decide Onboard Onboard Payroll Payroll Close

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: In a Strict Partial Order if there is no path between activities they are concurrent Here there is no edge between Interview and Decide Implication One can Make Hiring Decision before or parallel to Conduct Interviews

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Anomalies in Model 1 Decision before during Interview The decision can be made immediately after screening without waiting for interviews to complete This defeats the purpose of interviewing Interview Orphan The Interview activity does not lead to anything It runs parallel to the decision onboarding flow It does not influence the decision logically in the graph structure Missing Dependency Standard logic requires Interview to Decide This is missing

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Analyze POWL Model 2 Structure StrictPartialOrder containing Operators LOOP and XOR Nodes Post Screen Interview Decide loop onboarding xor payroll Close Operators loop onboarding LOOP Onboard skip xor payroll XOR Payroll skip Edges Post Screen Post Interview Interview Decide Decide loop onboarding loop onboarding xor payroll xor payroll Close

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This means Interview can happen immediately after Post bypassing Screen XOR Payroll skip means either Add to Payroll or do nothing LOOP Onboard skip implies Onboarding can be repeated

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Anomalies in Model 2 Parallel Screening Interviewing Post to Screen and Post to Interview implies you can Interview candidates before Screening them Optional Payroll XOR Payroll skip means you can onboard an employee and never add them to payroll Looping Onboarding LOOP Onboard skip implies re onboarding the same employee multiple times Missing Screen to Interview dependency Similar to Model 1 the link between Screening and Interviewing is weak

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Compare and Evaluate Model 1 preserves the mandatory nature of the steps no XOR skips for Payroll no Loops for Onboarding but gets the sequence wrong Decide before during Interview Model 2 gets the sequence of Decision Onboarding Payroll mostly right but introduces optionality Skip Payroll and repetition Loop Onboard which are semantically wrong for a single hire case

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Critical Failure In Model 1 the Interview activity is disconnected from the Decide activity You can hire without interviewing In Model 2 Payroll is optional A hired employee must be on payroll to get paid

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Re evaluating Model 1 Screen to Decide Screen to Interview There is no path from Interview to Decide This means the Decision is based only on Screening The Interview is performed but its result is not structurally required for the Decision Re evaluating Model 2 Interview to Decide The Decision waits for the Interview This is correct The anomaly is Post to Interview parallel to Screen The bigger issue is XOR Payroll Skip

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Model 1 has 1 major structural violation Interview Decide order Model 2 has 3 violations Screen Interview order Payroll mandatory Onboard singular Therefore Model 1 is arguably closer to normative if we assume the Interview edge was just drawn poorly but intended to be sequential whereas Model 2 explicitly introduces operators that change the nature of the activities making them optional or repeatable

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - PE
Text: You can argue that in some emergency hires onboarding starts while final interviews wrap up rare but possible You cannot argue that an employee works without being on payroll legal requirement

Name: Validation - PE
Text: In Model 1 all nodes usually must occur since there are no XORs So Interview must happen just not before Decide In Model 2 XOR explicitly allows skipping Payroll Missing a mandatory step Model 2 Payroll is generally a higher severity anomaly than wrong order Model 1 Interview Decide

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Conclusion Model 1 is preferable It enforces the completion of all critical steps including Payroll even if the sequencing of the selection phase is flawed Model 2 allows skipping critical compliance steps
